heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: No storm activity at the moment.  The previous storm occurred on 18 July 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The ongoing dry stretch, 19 days and counting started on 03 July 2026 and ranks 2nd in length since records started in 23 September 2024.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 3 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 27.7°C, observed on 24 April 2026 when the temperature fell to -4.5°C but also managed to reach 23.2°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is currently a heatwave underway.  It started on 2026-07-01 and has lasted 21 days.  So far this year, there has been 3 heatwaves.  Typically, the yearly average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 28 June 2026, there have been only 68 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (219°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: To date, the maximum wind gust recorded is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 2.8°C) and outside temperature (up 2.0°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 6%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 881 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 155 m) and light levels (down 2522 lux) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 881.3m lower than yesterday, 154.7m lower than the past week, 544.5m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1974m to a maximum of 8460m, averaging 4310m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 4460m and 3760m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will generally be on the hot side. Outside; hot temperatures throughout the day. Also, air moisture should feel ok and little or no wind. A few light raindrops may fall, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and very bright daylight. Little change in the weather.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
